_comment: The 95%-Wilson-lower-bound serve-time operating points for the ADOPTED conformal abstention hedge, one per certified adapter. Each is the max-coverage frontier point whose out-of-sample answered-top1 lower bound clears target_answered on the n=1024 cert. At serve time, abstain when the quant top1-top2-margin nonconformity exceeds `threshold` (no FP model needed). These are MEASURED selective accuracy on a held-out split, NOT per-token guarantees; canClaimAGI=false. Regenerate from a fresh cert via serving/abstention_serve.policy_from_cert(cert, confidence=0.95) if the adapter or serve distribution changes.

v6_downproj16.objective: KD + top1-margin output-space QAT + down_proj held bf16 (mixed precision)
v6_downproj16.regime: higher-coverage / higher-memory: +12.5pts coverage over v6 full-NVFP4 (0.7188->0.8438) at a memory cost (mem_ratio 3.30x->1.90x, ~42% less compression). Choose when memory budget allows; v6 (3.30x) stays the byte-efficient default.

note: v6 is the default (dominates v5: +8pts robust coverage). Both adapters raw-FAIL the 0.97 never-flip bar; the hedge serves the confident tokens and abstains on the low-margin argmax-flips. The `--keep-top-experts` mixed-precision lever (tools/expert_protection.py) is expected to raise coverage further (point-estimate ~0.93+) once cert-confirmed at n=1024. v6_downproj16 added (2026-07-01): down_proj-bf16 mixed precision lifts robust coverage to 0.8438 @ floor 0.9732 at mem 1.90x — a higher-coverage/higher-memory regime, NOT the default. keep-top-experts (Mac) may beat it on the coverage/memory trade.
